content.text: How do I convert a collection of raw text documents into a numerical document-term matrix using this vectorizer?

This function leverages a specialized transformation method from its parent class to handle the hashing and feature extraction process.

```python
def transform(self, Z):
        """Transform an ArrayRDD (or DictRDD with column 'X') containing
        sequence of documents to a document-term matrix.

        Parameters
        ----------
        Z : ArrayRDD or DictRDD with raw text documents
            Samples. Each sample must be a text document (either bytes or
            unicode strings) which will be tokenized and hashed.

        Returns
        -------
        Z : SparseRDD/DictRDD containg scipy.sparse matrix
            Document-term matrix.
        """
        mapper = super(SparkHashingVectorizer, self).transform
        return Z.transform(mapper, column='X', dtype=sp.spmatrix)
```

The resulting sparse matrix efficiently represents how often each term appears across all input documents without explicitly storing every word in memory.
If one of my input samples is already a pre-hashed numerical representation instead of raw text, will this function still generate the correct document-term matrix?

No, the hashing vectorizer expects raw string or byte data to perform tokenization and subsequent hashing. If your input is already encoded, you would need to ensure it matches what `SparkHashingVectorizer` expects.
Got it, so I need clean text inputs before passing them through this transformation.
